problem:
Filesystem MCP server modified .env without permission and did not back it up. **Describe the bug**
Claude Desktop (via the Filesystem MCP server) modified my .env file without consent when I asked a question about a Docker-related ConnectionRefusedError. It tried to “fix” a Tortoise ORM connection string issue by overwriting my file using the write_file command. However, it didn’t even read the .env file before modifying it, nor did it back it up. This led to loss of important config data I had (docker run --memory is not working — it seems correct per old docs)
memory_hint: Use --memory-reservation for resource limits. The old syntax was removed.
failure_without_memory: Keeps using docker run --memory which is deprecated. Endless debugging.
